provenance.notes: Migrated for 0.20.0 schema: advisor pass produced via the mandatory advisor agent path before provenance was a required field.

diffCoverage.notes: Reconciliation: code merged in f343624; the contract smoke is now run+attested and both required reviewers move to pass. Concur with security + architecture across the full changed-file set.

resolvedFindings.resolution: cred_store_roundtrip (real OS keychain + AES-GCM roundtrip + ciphertext-on-disk assertion) is now run and attested in the evidence bundle.
Evidence bundle status is pass with five attested+hash-verified checks (build/lint/tests/structural/smoke) and the three reviewer artifacts linked.
security-reviewer and architecture-reviewer both moved to pass after the live smoke + evidence bundle landed.

findings.evidence: crypto.rs AES-256-GCM seal/open is correct (random nonce, 32-byte keychain master key, opaque errors, truncated-blob guard, ciphertext-not-plaintext asserted); rusqlite/keyring/aes-gcm confined to store; no token across IPC; clean build/clippy -D warnings and offline tests pass. The live keychain roundtrip is now attested.

The 'relaunch GUI auto-restore' the earlier reviews mentioned is the session-restore feature's smoke, not secure-cred-store's contract smoke (cred_store_roundtrip). secure-cred-store proves credentials are encrypted at rest and round-trip; auto-restore-on-launch remains tracked under session-restore.
findings.fix: Complete session-restore separately with a device relaunch; non-blocking for secure-cred-store.

summary: secure-cred-store is honestly done after reconciliation. The code (merged in f343624) implements AES-256-GCM credential-at-rest under a single keychain master key with ciphertext-only SQLite storage, store-confined native deps, and no token across IPC. The previously-blocking gap — the unrun live smoke and missing pass-level reviews — is resolved: cred_store_roundtrip is now run and attested (ciphertext on disk, decrypts in memory) and both required reviewers move to pass. The plaintext-message-at-rest and keychain-key-loss risks are recorded as ADR-0005-accepted. Pass.
